Part B.key_evidence: Negative subtraction (isdiffpos=f) in g-band at mag ~20.8 with large sigma (0.21). The candidate is coincident (distpsnr1=0.30") with a PS1 source having roughly flat colors (g=19.77, r=19.88, i=19.85, z=19.66) and low sgscore1=0.16 suggesting a galaxy-like host, though classtar=0.64 and sharpnr~0 suggest point-like morphology in reference. Only 1 detection in history over 22 coverages. Difference cutout shows a faint, noisy residual without a clean PSF, and the template shows a visible source at center.
Part B.leading_interpretation_and_support: Most consistent with a marginal/bogus detection: a faint negative residual at low S/N (sigmapsf>0.2) on a source that is clearly present in the template. With ndethist=1 out of ncovhist=22 visits and a noisy difference cutout lacking a clean point-source residual, this is likely a subtraction artifact or noise fluctuation rather than a real astrophysical transient.
Part B.alternative_analysis: If real, the negative subtraction on a PS1-matched extended/ambiguous source could indicate low-level AGN or variable-star dimming. However, with only one detection over many epochs and marginal S/N, there is no supporting evidence for persistent variability, and the morphology/colors do not strongly favor AGN over a faint star.
